rationale: The cited paper is used in the core conceptual setup of the model: Li and Kannan explicitly justify their distinction between customer-initiated and firm-initiated channels with Bowman and Narayandas (2001). It is also used again in result interpretation, where the positive carryover of direct visits is linked to Bowman and Narayandas's finding that direct contacts can indicate stronger customer preference. This is more than a passing literature-list mention because it helps frame key constructs and interpret estimated effects.
relation_description: The citing paper applies ideas from Bowman and Narayandas (2001) to an online attribution context, especially the customer-initiated versus firm-initiated contact distinction and the interpretation of direct contact behavior. It uses that prior logic to motivate model structure and to explain empirical carryover findings.
